Air Quality.title: Air Quality
Air Quality.description:
Measures the cleanliness of the air a population breathes and how pollution evolves over time.

Influences:
  • Health outcomes such as respiratory and cardiovascular disease risk
  • Population vulnerability to pollution-related illness
  • Climate stress when combined with heat or humidity
  • Socioeconomic burden on healthcare systems in affected areas

Climate.title: Climate Conditions
Climate.description:
Captures the full spectrum of weather patterns, temperature extremes, and climate anomalies in a region.

Influences:
  • Risk of heat-related illness and mortality during extreme events
  • Flood exposure and infrastructure resilience
  • Agricultural productivity and food security
  • Air pollution behavior and dispersion
  • Seasonal demand on healthcare systems

Socioeconomic.title: Socioeconomic Status
Socioeconomic.description:
Reflects the economic strength and social welfare of a population, shaping how well communities can cope with environmental pressures.

Influences:
  • Ability to access quality healthcare and preventive services
  • Resilience to food insecurity during climate shocks
  • Exposure to pollution in lower-income, industrialized areas
  • Long-term adaptive capacity to environmental and health risks

Healthcare.title: Healthcare Access & Capacity
Healthcare.description:
Describes the availability and quality of medical infrastructure and how well it can respond to environmental health threats.

Influences:
  • Population survival rates during heat waves and pollution events
  • Speed of recovery from climate-driven health crises
  • Burden placed on health systems by vulnerable groups
  • Disparity in health outcomes across regions

Seasonal.title: Seasonal & Temporal Patterns
Seasonal.description:
Encodes how conditions shift cyclically throughout the year, capturing rhythms that repeat across months and quarters.

Influences:
  • Predictability of pollution peaks and temperature swings
  • Timing of agricultural cycles and food availability
  • Seasonal strain on energy grids and healthcare systems
  • Short-term environmental forecasting and trend detection

Geographic & Spatial.title: Geography & Spatial Context
Geographic & Spatial.description:
Defines where a location sits in the world and how its surroundings shape its environmental conditions.

Influences:
  • Baseline climate zone and temperature regime
  • Susceptibility to tropical diseases and extreme weather
  • How pollution and heat from neighboring regions spill over locally
  • Inequality in environmental exposure across hemispheres and latitudes

Exposure & Risk.title: Exposure & Risk Profile
Exposure & Risk.description:
Aggregates the most critical risk factors to paint a picture of how vulnerable a population is to environmental and climate hazards.

Influences:
  • Overall population resilience and adaptive capacity
  • Likelihood of compounded crises — e.g. heat waves alongside poor air quality
  • Priority targeting for public health interventions
  • Long-term mortality and morbidity projections under climate change scenarios

Others.title: Other Factors
Others.description: Other factors that represent influences on the system.
